risk: May cause symptom patching, oversearching, or asking 'which error?' instead of acting.
transformation_principle: Preserve current error/log context. Direct root-cause fix with smallest safe change, verification, and reporting.
compact_rewrite_template: Using the current error/log/project context, identify the root cause. Apply the smallest safe fix, verify where practical, and report the cause, files changed, and checks performed.

risk: May repeat previous fix attempt that failed, or suggest unrelated changes.
transformation_principle: Acknowledge recurrence. Check what was tried before. Find what changed. Fix root cause, not symptom.
compact_rewrite_template: Using the current error context and any previous fix attempts in session history, determine whether the error is the same or different. If the same, check what changed since the last attempt. Apply the smallest corrected fix, verify, and report findings.

risk: May invent a fake plan if expanded unnecessarily.
transformation_principle: Minimal expansion (1-15 tokens). Trust session context. Just signal to proceed.

risk: May repeat completed steps or invent details if session context is thin.
transformation_principle: Reference existing plan without restating it. Verify current progress before proceeding.
compact_rewrite_template: Continue from the current plan/previous discussion. Verify what has been completed so far, then proceed with the next uncompleted step. Make the smallest safe progress, verify where practical, and report what was done and what remains.

risk: May overengineer by adding multiple sub-features, inventing tech stack, or refactoring unrelated code.
transformation_principle: Scope to the stated feature only. Use existing tech stack and project conventions. Make minimal changes to add the feature. No surrounding cleanup unless directly related.
compact_rewrite_template: Implement the requested feature using the existing project stack and conventions. Add only the minimum code needed. Avoid unrelated refactors, dependency additions, or architectural changes. Verify where practical and report files changed.

risk: May introduce unnecessary rewrites or 'improvements' that change behavior or break tests.
transformation_principle: Scope refactor narrowly. Preserve all existing behavior and tests. Prefer small, incremental improvements over large rewrites.
compact_rewrite_template: Refactor the specified code to improve readability and maintainability while preserving all existing behavior. Keep changes minimal. Do not rewrite what works. Add or update tests if behavior-relevant paths change. Verify and report files changed.

risk: May trigger full rewrite, add unnecessary error handling everywhere, or invent deployment infrastructure.
transformation_principle: Audit for gaps without rewriting. Add error handling only at system boundaries. Do not bloat internal code with defensive checks for impossible states.
compact_rewrite_template: Audit the current code for production readiness gaps. Add error handling at system boundaries only. Do not add defensive checks for impossible states. Verify existing test coverage. Make minimal targeted improvements and report findings.

risk: May superficially patch tests to pass without fixing root cause, or add irrelevant tests.
transformation_principle: Fix root cause first, then fix tests. When adding tests, cover happy path, edge cases, and the specific bug path. Use existing test patterns.
compact_rewrite_template: Identify why tests are failing: root cause in code or test itself. Fix root cause first, then ensure tests validate the fix. Follow existing test patterns in the project. Add tests covering the fix path. Verify all tests pass and report findings.

risk: May invent deployment provider, cloud platform, or CI system that does not exist in the project.
transformation_principle: Use existing deployment config and CI setup. Do not invent deployment providers. Fix what exists, do not replace.
compact_rewrite_template: Using the existing deployment/CI configuration in the project, diagnose and fix the issue. Do not add new deployment providers or platforms. Make minimal config changes. Verify with existing deploy scripts and report findings.

risk: May commit code that has not been verified, skip safety checks, or commit with poor messages.
transformation_principle: Verify changes first. Review for safety (secrets, debug code, destructive ops). Write a clear commit message summarizing what and why. Commit only if safe.
compact_rewrite_template: Review all current changes for safety: check for secrets, debug code, destructive operations, and incomplete features. Verify tests pass. Write a concise commit message explaining what changed and why. Commit only if no safety issues found. Report what was committed or why not.

risk: May rewrite entire UI components or add unnecessary styling frameworks.
transformation_principle: Target the specific UI element or behavior mentioned. Use existing styling approach. No rewrites of unrelated components.
compact_rewrite_template: Fix the specific UI element or behavior described. Use the existing styling framework and component patterns. Make minimal CSS/component changes. Verify appearance where practical and report files changed.

risk: May invent database, auth provider, or API design that conflicts with existing project architecture.
transformation_principle: Extend existing backend patterns. Use same database, same auth approach, same API conventions. Do not switch providers.
compact_rewrite_template: Add the requested backend functionality following the existing project patterns: same database, same auth approach, same API conventions. Extend rather than replace. Verify where practical and report files changed.

risk: May overengineer with unnecessary complexity or invent stack choices not aligned with user's context.
transformation_principle: Ask what the user has or prefers. Do not assume a stack. Propose minimal viable architecture first.
compact_rewrite_template: Propose a minimal viable architecture using what the user has stated or what the project already uses. Prefer simple over scalable-first. State tradeoffs but do not overbuild. Request confirmation before implementing.
must_not_invent: frameworks the user did not mention

risk: May introduce premature optimizations, complex caching, or micro-optimizations that hurt readability.
transformation_principle: Profile first, optimize second. Measure before and after. Prefer algorithmic improvements over caching or micro-optimizations.
compact_rewrite_template: Profile the code to identify the actual bottleneck before optimizing. Measure before and after each change. Prefer algorithmic improvements. Add caching only if profiling confirms it is needed. Verify behavior preserved and report improvements with metrics.

risk: May invent auth provider (Stripe, Auth0, Clerk) or over-implement security beyond what is needed.
transformation_principle: Use what the project already has. If no auth exists, propose minimal approach. Never invent specific auth providers.
compact_rewrite_template: Add the requested security/authentication feature using the project's existing approach. If no auth system exists yet, propose the simplest option. Do not invent specific providers unless the user named one. Verify and report files changed.

risk: May expand into a full plan when user just wants to continue or confirm.
transformation_principle: Pass through unchanged when clearly conversational.

risk: May repeat same failed approach.
transformation_principle: Acknowledge and retry with lesson from previous attempt. 1-15 token expansion.

risk: May rewrite or interpret slash commands as text prompts, breaking agent command processing.
transformation_principle: Pass through unchanged. Slash commands are agent/client commands and must not be rewritten.

raw_patterns: any prompt that is already specific, contextual, and actionable
risk: Rewriting a clear prompt risks introducing ambiguity or losing user's original intent.
transformation_principle: Pass through unchanged. Detect 'already good' by checking if prompt is contextual, has specifics, and is actionable without further clarification.

risk: May over-document by explaining obvious code, or add transient task context to permanent docs.
transformation_principle: Document WHAT and WHY the code exists, not HOW it works. No transient task context in permanent docs. Follow existing doc patterns.
compact_rewrite_template: Document the specified code following the project's existing documentation patterns. Explain what the code does and why it exists, not how it works line-by-line. Do not include transient task context, issue numbers, or flow names. Report files changed.

raw_patterns: I think I have broken you
risk: May trigger full debug cycle when user just wants acknowledgment and continuation.
transformation_principle: Minimal acknowledgment and continue. Trust session context.
